_readme: Executable form of ticket-plan-2026-07-21.md. Consumed by deploy/ops/create-tickets.sh via the tickets.yml workflow. Order matters: {{key}} placeholders resolve to owner/repo#number of EARLIER entries. Delete alongside the plan doc once executed.

tickets.title: M2: Nactor credential-scope reader — deliver credentials as Director-signed grants
tickets.body:
## Context
The credential-grant migration's delivery half is built-but-never-used (docs/migration-status-2026-07.md — the night-of-drift correction); consumption (broker, 5 providers) is live. The missing piece is the reader: Nactor, for each runtime identity, fetches + NIP-44-decrypts credential grants addressed to it and derives entitlements from grants instead of nave.env. nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §3/§5 calls this "the one missing piece" — it unblocks M3–M7 AND warm.contact/Quill (per-user hierarchy, nave.pub docs/quill.md §5).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Reader module + tests (fetch, decrypt, validate grant → entitlement)
- [ ] Entitlements sourced from grants when present; env fallback flagged in logs
- [ ] Grants surfaced in the runtime audit (AD-1) and /api/state entitlements
- [ ] credential-consumption-policy.md + migration-status doc updated

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5 (M-series).

tickets.title: M3 pilot: deliver telegram-luke as a real grant; retire its env copy
tickets.body:
First credential through the delivery path end-to-end: Director issues the scoped grant → Nactor reads it ({{N1}}) → Luke's comms bot works with the env copy removed.

- [ ] Grant issued + read live
- [ ] Env copy removed; Luke comms verified working
- [ ] Rollback path documented

Depends on {{N1}}. Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: M4: migrate remaining credentials to grants (gworkspace, anthropic, approval bots)
tickets.body:
Repeat the {{N2}} pattern for the rest of the live credentials; retire env copies one by one; runtime audit shows each migration.

- [ ] gworkspace
- [ ] anthropic
- [ ] telegram approval bots
- [ ] nave.env carries no credential a grant now delivers

Depends on {{N2}}. Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: M5: mail connector — verb-scoped read-only IMAP (Nmail)
tickets.body:
## Context
The first real connector (the docs' #36 — that number predates this tracker; recreated here). Pinned design: `stateful-adapter` × {app-password | OAuth}, verb-scoped **READ-ONLY** IMAP enforced at the protocol, `POST /api/connector/mail`. Takes the Gmail app password off disk into RAM-only custody; also unblocks warm.contact's Gmail path (grant-to-app).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] stateful-adapter with app-password and OAuth modes
- [ ] Read-only verb set enforced at the protocol layer (no send, no delete)
- [ ] POST /api/connector/mail behind NIP-98
- [ ] Gmail app password removed from disk

Depends on {{N1}}. Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5 (M5); docs/imap-adapter.
tickets._note: was tracked as nact#36 in the docs; that number does not exist in this tracker

tickets.title: M6: engine egress → /api/proxy (Anthropic key leaves the engine env)
tickets.body:
Point the OpenClaw engine's Anthropic calls at the transparent egress proxy (/api/proxy/<provider>, credential injected from RAM) so the engine never holds the key. The proxy organ exists (NCP v0, nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §3); this is the cutover.

- [ ] Engine egress via proxy in prod
- [ ] Key removed from engine env
- [ ] Latency/error budget checked before+after

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5 (M6).

tickets.title: M7: Nvoy MCP transport for grant delivery
tickets.body:
Deliver/receive credential grants over the Nvoy MCP mount so agents and consoles share one transport instead of bespoke relay reads.

- [ ] Grant fetch/decrypt available as MCP tool calls
- [ ] Nactor reader ({{N1}}) can consume via this transport

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5 (M7).

tickets.title: Hardening P1: freeze created_at at propose; re-verify event-id fingerprint before signing
tickets.body:
WYSIWYS: the event a human approved must be byte-identical to the event signed. Freeze created_at at propose time; recompute and compare the event-id fingerprint immediately before signing; refuse on mismatch.

- [ ] created_at frozen at propose
- [ ] Fingerprint re-verified at sign; mismatch rejects + audits
- [ ] Tamper test: a mutated queued event cannot be signed

Source: threat model (docs/), nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5 (hardening phases).

tickets.title: Hardening P2: faithful render — kind/tags/hidden-character flags in approval surfaces
tickets.body:
Approval cards render exactly what will be signed: kind, full tags, and warnings for hidden/bidi/homoglyph characters — no summarized rendering that can hide payload.

- [ ] Kind + full tags visible on every card (web + telegram)
- [ ] Hidden-char/bidi/homoglyph detection with visible flags
- [ ] Adversarial fixtures render with warnings

Source: threat model; nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: Hardening P3: risk tiers — critical kinds cannot be one-tap approved
tickets.body:
Tier event kinds by blast radius; critical ones (key rotation, grant issuance, profile/relay-list changes) require a stronger ceremony than one tap (hold-to-confirm / second factor / web-queue only).

- [ ] Tier table documented
- [ ] Enforcement in the approval flow
- [ ] Tests: critical kind refuses one-tap path

Source: threat model; nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: Hardening P4: channel binding as a scoped grant (nonce ceremony)
tickets.body:
Bind approval channels to identities via a nonce ceremony recorded as a scoped grant, so a hijacked bot or chat cannot impersonate the approver.

- [ ] Nonce ceremony design + implementation
- [ ] Binding recorded as a grant; revocable by rotation

Source: threat model; nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: Hardening P5: Mini-App signer at nact.nave.pub/sign
tickets.body:
The in-Telegram Mini-App signer surface behind the nostr gate, honoring risk tiers ({{N8}}).

- [ ] /sign Mini-App behind nostr-signed gate
- [ ] Risk tiers respected (critical kinds keep their stronger ceremony)

Source: threat model; nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: NCP: per-identity gate on /api/proxy
tickets.body:
The v0 egress proxy injects credentials but does not yet scope callers per identity. Add NIP-98 per-identity gating and a per-identity provider allowlist derived from entitlements.

- [ ] NIP-98 auth on /api/proxy
- [ ] Provider allowlist per identity (from entitlements)
- [ ] Denials audited

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §3 (NCP open items).

tickets.title: NCP: data-grant read path (+ optional MCP-resource front)
tickets.body:
The perceive-side "missing quadrant": let an on-box agent read NIP-DA scoped data sets it holds grants for, via NCP; optionally front them as MCP resources.

- [ ] Design pass on docs/ncp.md
- [ ] Thin v0: fetch + decrypt scoped data for a held grant
- [ ] Optional: MCP resource front

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §3/§5.

tickets.title: Nops v0 design spike: swap relay-ops' SSH transport for grant + signed approval
tickets.body:
Per docs/nops.md and nave.pub docs/NOPS.md: today's restricted relay-ops allowlist is proto-Nops over the wrong transport. Spec the swap: the ops-runner holds its own identity; allowed verbs arrive as a scoped grant; exec happens on signed approval; no SSH key, no CI secret.

- [ ] Design doc: identity, verb-grant schema, approval flow, revocation
- [ ] Explicit mapping from the current ci-ops-allow.sh verbs
- [ ] Build gated on James's sign-off

Source: nact/docs/nops.md; nave.pub docs/NOPS.md.

tickets.title: Wire nave-connect into every app login + unified title bar
tickets.body:
The nave-connect module is built + tested; the bunker path is proven (Armada). Wire it into each app's login UI and the unified title bar. Nvoy keeps its local-key onboarding (gated "advanced"); Nact stays signer-only (James 2026-07-18).

- [ ] Per-app wiring checklist (nvoy, nvelope, nontact, notegate, nherit, noir, ntrigue, hub)
- [ ] Unified title bar
- [ ] Nvoy local-key onboarding preserved

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5 (#56 build direction).

tickets.title: Delegate approval authority James → Nact_jaf (signature-gated)
tickets.body:
Formalize the delegation so Nact_jaf can carry approvals on James's behalf where configured (the docs' #48 — number predates this tracker). Signature-gated and James-only: the delegation itself is a Director-signed act; nothing executes without it.

- [ ] Delegation grant/event design (scope, expiry)
- [ ] Revocation = rotation documented
- [ ] James signs; runtime audit shows the chain

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5; docs/nave-architecture-decisions.md (queued builds).

tickets.title: Hierarchical re-grant: confirm a grantee identity can re-issue scoped sub-grants
tickets.body:
The Quill linchpin (nave.pub docs/quill.md §5 — user→Quill is the simplest one-hop case) and the assumption under the central-identity fleet model (warm.contact integration review §6).

- [ ] Determine whether kinds 30440/440/441/10440 as specced support a grantee re-issuing a narrower grant today
- [ ] Decide revocation-cascade semantics (does rotating the mid-key kill descendants?)
- [ ] Prototype one-hop user→instance re-grant
- [ ] Feed conclusions to SPEC FUTURE.md (attenuation north star, SPEC §10) and the warm.contact bootstrap ticket

Related: {{N1}} (the reader that consumes such grants).

tickets.title: Grant migration M1+M2: credentials as first-class grants in the Ledger
tickets.body:
The Nvoy half of the credential-grant migration (the docs' #37, in-flight — number predates this tracker). Represent credential grants first-class in the Ledger and the issuance flow, aligned with the Nactor reader ({{N1}}).

- [ ] M1: Ledger models credential grants distinctly from data grants
- [ ] M2: issuance flow for Director-signed credential grants

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5 (Nvoy).

tickets.title: A2: credential ciphertext → owning identity
tickets.body:
Move credential ciphertext custody to the owning identity (the docs' #43): the grant addressed to the identity holds the secret; Nactor's redundant copies get revoked after cross-box value-sourcing.

- [ ] Ciphertext addressed to the owning identity only
- [ ] Redundant copies revoked once consumers cut over

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5; nave-architecture-decisions.md (migration finish-work).

tickets.title: luke.env → nave.env: retire the aliases
tickets.body:
Finish the env rename (the docs' #44): drop the luke.env aliases once no consumer references them. Sequenced deliberately so a late change can't strand the live box.

- [ ] Consumer audit shows zero luke.env references
- [ ] Aliases removed; deploy verified

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: Fleet console: central-identity view
tickets.body:
The central-identity fleet console (the docs' #59), from the warm.contact review: one view of identities, standing grants, and instances under the Director.

- [ ] Console lists identities + standing grants
- [ ] Revocation (rotation) action surfaced

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: Re-delegation terms end-to-end
tickets.body:
Carry re-delegation terms through issuance → display → enforcement (the docs' #60); pairs with {{V1}}.

- [ ] Terms expressed at issuance
- [ ] Shown in the Ledger; honored by consumers

Source: nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5.

tickets.title: Shepherd PR nostr-protocol/nips#2411
tickets.body:
Standing ticket for the NIP-DA upstream PR: respond to review as it comes; concede bikesheds (kind numbers, tag names); defend invariants (revocation-by-rotation, zero relay changes, gift-wrapped grants, one-key recovery); consider a constructive comment on the complementary PR #2258; attach the Nvoy 90-second revoke-mid-conversation demo when the thread warms.

Log shepherding activity as comments here so the history is in one place.

Source: nave.pub docs/JOURNEY.md open ledger.

tickets.title: Quill: per-user identity bootstrap — mint-or-BYO Director; mint Quill npub; issue the scoped grant
tickets.body:
nave.pub docs/quill.md §3/§6: at signup, mint the user a nostr identity (or accept a BYO npub for the nostr-native minority) — the user is the Director. Mint that user's Quill npub, register it, and have the Director issue the scoped grant (profile bundle + credentials).

- [ ] Mint-or-BYO flow at signup (no nostr knowledge required for mint path)
- [ ] Quill npub minted + registered per user
- [ ] Scoped grant issued; revocation = rotation verified

Depends on {{N1}} (grant reader pattern) and {{V1}} (one-hop re-grant confirmation).

tickets.title: Quill: Swift grant plumbing — NIP-44 decrypt + NIP-98 sign; grant-backed SecretVault source
tickets.body:
nave.pub docs/quill.md §5: add swift-secp256k1-based NIP-44 decrypt and NIP-98 signing to the notarized app; implement a new SecretVault source — "fetch + decrypt the grant addressed to this Quill's npub" — with zero calling-code changes (SecretVault is already the indirection). Custody: Keychain WhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly.

- [ ] NIP-44 decrypt + NIP-98 sign in WarmCore
- [ ] Grant-backed SecretVault implementation
- [ ] Existing engine tests still green (59)

tickets.title: Quill: add calendlyURL to ReconnectProfile; surface in coffee/meetup CTA
tickets.body:
The one net-new profile field (nave.pub docs/quill.md §4): a booking link so coffee/meetup drafts close with a real time instead of "let's find a time". Small and shippable now — no dependency on the identity work.

- [ ] calendlyURL on ReconnectProfile + profile editor
- [ ] CTA uses it only for coffee/meetup intents
- [ ] Guardrail tests extended (no link on hello-only intents)

tickets.title: Quill: per-user npub lifecycle at scale — register / scope / revoke (design)
tickets.body:
nave.pub docs/quill.md §6 open item: how many per-user Quill npubs are registered, scoped, rate-limited, and revoked at scale — relay allowlisting policy, abuse handling, cleanup on account deletion.

- [ ] Design doc covering registration, scoping, revocation, deletion
- [ ] Relay policy for per-user identities decided

tickets.title: Nave integration: live Director grant → warm.contact npub; real Swift MCP transport (D-N1/D-N2)
tickets.body:
The decided v0.5 §10 integration (grant-to-app via Nvoy MCP; central identity minted): stand up the real path — a live Director-signed grant to the warm.contact identity, a real MCP transport in Swift, per-instance topology per D-N1/D-N2.

- [ ] Live grant issued to the warm.contact npub
- [ ] Swift MCP transport (not the stub)
- [ ] D-N1/D-N2 topology decision recorded

tickets.title: Google People sync (behind the brand-verification gate)
tickets.body:
Two-way sync with Google People, gated on the brand-verification prerequisite. Zero-knowledge invariant holds: the relay only ever brokers ciphertext; sync runs client-side.

Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: Living contacts: profile-key pull
tickets.body: Pull updates for contacts that shared a profile key, so cards stay current without anyone re-sending. Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: Multi-device: per-device tokens
tickets.body: Per-device tokens so a second device doesn't clone the first's credential; revoke per device. Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: CAPTCHA on the wave-in flow
tickets.body: Abuse control on inbound waves without breaking the zero-knowledge posture. Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: Custom domains
tickets.body: Let users serve their wave-in page from their own domain. Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: Billing
tickets.body: Payment rails + plan gating. Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: Launch loop
tickets.body: The activation/launch cadence: onboarding polish, invite mechanics, feedback capture. Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: Daily-brief agent
tickets.body: The unbuilt daily-brief agent: who waved, who's waiting on you, what Quill drafted overnight. Pairs with the Quill queue (ReconnectPriority). Source: warm.contact backlog (nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §5).

tickets.title: Employment roadmap (epic)
tickets.body:
From the open ledger (nave.pub docs/JOURNEY.md). Checklist epic — split items into child issues as they start:

- [ ] CRM loop: Epiq / HG / Generation / Insulet prep briefs + reconnection cadence
- [ ] Phone-node pairing
- [ ] WhatsApp: family + Esterones
- [ ] Tour watcher
- [ ] World-models digest
- [ ] BJJ log
- [ ] The Nostr channel

(#26 console authoring pass 2 stays parked — deliberately not re-ticketed.)

tickets.title: M3: the AI Director
tickets.body:
nave.pub docs/INVENTORY.md §2a: Noir is M1 of 6 with M3 (AI Director) in progress — the Director is itself an nvoy agent; grants are earned intel; a key rotation is a felt burn notice.

- [ ] Director runs as an nvoy agent under a scoped grant
- [ ] Clue issuance = grant issuance; burn = rotation
- [ ] One full mystery playable against the AI Director

tickets.title: Review the six autonomous design decisions + legal/brand language pass
tickets.body:
Six autonomous design decisions from the build handoff (§5 of its handoff doc) await James's review, plus a legal/brand language pass before wider release.

- [ ] Each decision accepted or reversed, with a note
- [ ] Legal/brand language reviewed
- [ ] SECURITY.md revocation-physics wording confirmed

Source: nave.pub docs/JOURNEY.md open ledger.

tickets.title: Deploy the Cloudflare MC proxy (optional)
tickets.body:
The Cloudflare MC proxy is built but not deployed; keyless mode works today, so this is optional by design. Deploy + document, or explicitly close as won't-do.

Source: nave.pub docs/JOURNEY.md open ledger.
